TodoList_Version.conversation_history: I'm a cs undergrad at uoft and plan for grad school application recently. give me a 1 year todo list

TodoList.title: 1-Year Grad School Application To-Do List
TodoList.overview: Monthly milestones and concrete tasks to prepare, apply, and follow up for graduate school applications over the next 12 months.

Task.name: Clarify goals and programs
Task.description: Decide target fields (CS subareas), degree type (MS/PhD), and 6-10 target programs.

check_items.check_item: Rank programs by fit and admission timeline

Task.name: Create application calendar
Task.description: Map deadlines, test dates, and internal milestones over 12 months.

check_items.check_item: Set reminders 8 and 4 weeks before each deadline

Task.name: Assess standardized tests
Task.description: Decide if GRE/subject tests are needed and plan prep.

Task.name: Identify coursework gaps
Task.description: Find prerequisite classes or skills to strengthen candidacy.

check_items.check_item: Plan to take courses or online modules

Task.name: Engage in research / summer project planning
Task.description: Start or ramp up research/projects to strengthen statement and letters.

Task.name: Document results
Task.description: Keep clear notes, code repo, and a short write-up for each project.

check_items.check_item: Prepare short README and results summary

Task.name: Build list of potential advisors
Task.description: Identify 10-20 faculty per program whose work aligns with yours.

check_items.check_item: Note specific projects to mention in emails

Task.name: Start polite outreach
Task.description: Email faculty with concise interest and relevant work; request advice.

Task.name: Draft personal statement / SOP
Task.description: Write an initial draft focusing on research experience, fit, and goals.

Task.name: Update academic CV
Task.description: Create a concise CV highlighting research, publications, and relevant coursework.

Task.name: Identify recommenders
Task.description: Choose 3-4 people familiar with your research and academic potential.

check_items.check_item: Prefer research supervisors and senior instructors

Task.name: Request recommendations
Task.description: Ask recommenders early, provide deadlines and materials.

check_items.check_item: Send CV, statement draft, and key points
Confirm willingness and expected submission date

Task.name: Intensive test prep (if needed)
Task.description: Follow a study plan for GRE/subject tests; take practice exams weekly.

Task.name: Collect unofficial transcripts
Task.description: Download transcripts to include with pre-applications or for recommenders.

Task.name: Revise SOP and CV based on feedback
Task.description: Incorporate advisor and peer feedback; tailor versions for specific programs.

Task.name: Prepare writing/research samples
Task.description: Select/format papers or project reports to submit or share with recommenders.

check_items.check_item: Polish one-page summary for each sample

Task.name: Create accounts and pre-fill forms
Task.description: Open application portals, upload documents and ensure recommenders have links.

check_items.check_item: Enter basic info in each portal
Upload CV and transcripts where allowed

Task.name: Draft short answers and program-specific statements
Task.description: Prepare concise responses for program prompts and questions.

Task.name: Complete final reviews
Task.description: Proofread, verify recommenders, double-check transcripts and fees.

check_items.check_item: Verify PDFs open and are labeled correctly

Task.name: Submit applications
Task.description: Pay fees and submit; save confirmation emails and receipts.

Task.name: Prepare for interviews
Task.description: Practice research talks, common questions, and technical anchors.

Task.name: Send thank-you/follow-up notes
Task.description: Send concise emails to interviewers and faculty contacts.

Task.name: Compare offers and funding
Task.description: Evaluate advisor fit, funding, location, and long-term goals.

Task.name: Plan contingencies
Task.description: If no offers, plan reapplication or gap-year research options.
